findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that read environment variables (API keys, tokens, secrets) and transmit them externally. Common in supply chain attacks for credential theft.
findings.recommendation: This code reads environment variables and appears to transmit them externally. This is a critical credential theft pattern. Do not install.

matches.description: Environment variables sent in POST request body

findings.ruleDescription: Detects direct access to known sensitive credential files. Covers AWS credentials, SSH keys, GPG keys, .env files, browser credential stores, and system password files.
findings.recommendation: This code directly accesses known credential storage files (AWS keys, SSH keys, .env, browser stores). Legitimate MCP servers and skills should never need to read these files. This is a strong indicator of credential theft.

findings.ruleTitle: Pipeline taint chain — data read + transform + exfiltrate
findings.ruleDescription: Detects multi-step exfiltration chains where sensitive data is read, optionally transformed/obfuscated, then sent over the network. Inspired by Cisco's pipeline taint tracking system. The combination of credential access + encoding + network send is the classic exfiltration chain.
findings.recommendation: This code reads sensitive data (credentials, environment variables), transforms or encodes it, then sends it over the network. This is the classic data exfiltration pipeline. Multiple detection rules triggered together confirm this is intentional credential theft, not coincidental pattern matches.

matches.description: Read sensitive data, encode, send in one expression
matches.snippet: Readable as He}from"stream";import Qt from"crypto";var _=class extends Error{constructor(e,t){super(e,t),this.name="RequestError"}},Mt=e=>e instanceof _?e:new _(e.message,{cause:e}),Nt=global.Request,

matches.description: Environment variable directly sent to network

location.snippet: Readable as He}from"stream";import Qt from"crypto";var _=class extends Error{constructor(e,t){super(e,t),this.name="RequestError"}},Mt=e=>e instanceof _?e:new _(e.message,{cause:e}),Nt=global.Request,

findings.ruleDescription: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.
findings.reportTitle: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation
findings.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

matches.description: Lines exceeding 3000 chars in source code suggest packed/obfuscated content

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.
findings.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

matches.description: exec/spawn with template literal containing variables

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.

matchDetails.description: exec/spawn with template literal containing variables

properties.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

matches.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

fullDescription.text: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.

matchDetails.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

properties.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type
